panels.description: Summary of protection flags. protected=1 means a backup restore point was found. no_backup=1 means this VM is known to the collector but no backup restore point was found. stale_backup/stale_replication indicate stale or missing recent protection.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "veeam_vm_protection")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "protected" or r._field == "no_backup" or r._field == "stale_backup" or r._field == "stale_replication")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.customer =~ /^${customer:regex}$/ and r.site =~ /^${site:regex}$/ and r.server =~ /^${server:regex}$/)
  |> last()

panels.title: Protection Summary

panels.description: This is the main list for VMs not being backed up. A VM can only appear here if Veeam exposes it through inventory, restore points, task sessions, or replication jobs.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "veeam_vm_protection")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "no_backup" and r._value == 1)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.customer =~ /^${customer:regex}$/ and r.site =~ /^${site:regex}$/ and r.server =~ /^${server:regex}$/)
  |> group(columns:["vm", "protection_status", "protection_type"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns:["_time", "vm", "protection_status", "protection_type", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns:{_value:"No backup"})

panels.title: VMs With No Backup

panels.description: VMs with stale backup or stale replication flags.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "veeam_vm_protection")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "stale_backup" or r._field == "stale_replication")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._value == 1)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.customer =~ /^${customer:regex}$/ and r.site =~ /^${site:regex}$/ and r.server =~ /^${server:regex}$/)
  |> group(columns:["vm", "protection_type", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns:["_time", "vm", "protection_type", "_field", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns:{_field:"Stale flag", _value:"Is stale"})

panels.title: Stale Protection

panels.description: Full VM protection table. Set the VM variable or open the VM dashboard to drill into a specific VM.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "veeam_vm_protection")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.vm =~ /^${vm:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.customer =~ /^${customer:regex}$/ and r.site =~ /^${site:regex}$/ and r.server =~ /^${server:regex}$/)
  |> group(columns:["vm", "protection_status", "protection_type", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> group(columns:["vm", "protection_status", "protection_type"])
  |> pivot(rowKey:["vm", "protection_status", "protection_type"], columnKey:["_field"], valueColumn:"_value")
  |> keep(columns:["vm", "protection_status", "protection_type", "protected", "no_backup", "backup", "backup_copy", "replication", "stale_backup", "stale_replication", "restore_point_count", "latest_restore_point_age_hours"])

panels.title: All VM Protection Detail
